SessionStart.command: echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"SessionStart","additionalContext":"Read user/profile.md now. If it contains unfilled template placeholders, use AskUserQuestion to interview the user section by section and populate each section (see Session Start in CLAUDE.md). If already filled out but incomplete, ask the user to fill in the gaps before proceeding. Then check MCP tool availability."}}'

FW=$(cat .framework-version 2>/dev/null); [ -z "$FW" ] && exit 0; PV=$(jq -r '.version' ~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/personas/plugins/persona-manager/.claude-plugin/plugin.json 2>/dev/null); [ -z "$PV" ] && exit 0; [ "$FW" = "$PV" ] && exit 0; echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"SessionStart","additionalContext":"Framework update available ('"$FW"' -> '"$PV"'). Say update persona to sync."}}'

Stop.prompt: Before ending: reflect on whether anything meaningful happened this session (decisions, corrections, discoveries, preferences). Native auto-memory handles persistence automatically — do NOT manually create or edit files in user/memory/. Just ensure important insights surface in your thinking so auto-memory captures them. Skip if nothing worth remembering occurred.

PreCompact.prompt: Context is about to be compacted. Reflect on any important session context that should be preserved — decisions, discoveries, user preferences, corrections. Native auto-memory handles persistence automatically. Do NOT manually create or edit files in user/memory/.

PostCompact.command: echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PostCompact","additionalContext":"Context was just compacted. Check user/memory/.last-crash — if it exists, a previous session ended unexpectedly. Read it, offer to review what was lost, then delete the marker."}}'
